methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

transformations: hooksData parsed as provider address + credential bytes
data_flows.trust_changes: AccessControlHooks.onQueueWithdrawal has NO isHooked check, unlike FixedTermLoanHooks. Any address can call this function as msg.sender, triggering credential validation and storage writes.
exposure_risks: Lender credential state can be modified by unauthenticated callers
manipulation_risks: Attacker can call onQueueWithdrawal from any address, triggering _tryValidateAccess which writes to _lenderStatus storage
Attacker can force expiration of a lender's credential by calling when credential is expired, causing unsetCredential to be written to storage
Attacker can trigger stateful validateCredential calls on providers via crafted hooksData without the lender's consent

transformations: fixedTermEndTime: 256-bit calldataload assigned to uint32 variable in assembly
data_flows.trust_changes: Borrower-supplied calldata parsed via inline assembly without Solidity type-safety guarantees
validation_points: fixedTermEndTime range check against block.timestamp and MaximumLoanTerm
exposure_risks: fixedTermEndTime uses full 256-bit value in validation but is stored as uint32 - truncation mismatch possible for future timestamps beyond uint32 max
manipulation_risks: Currently safe due to block.timestamp fitting in uint32, but will break after year 2106 when block.timestamp exceeds uint32.max

path: Return value read via assembly (mload(0) masked to uint32)
transformations: Provider return value masked to uint32 via and(mload(0), 0xffffffff)
Expiry calculated as satAdd(credentialTimestamp, timeToLive, type(uint32).max)
data_flows.trust_changes: External untrusted contract return data is used to set credential timestamps. Provider address is validated against _roleProviders mapping.
exposure_risks: Malicious provider could return manipulated timestamps to extend or shorten credential validity
manipulation_risks: Provider returning credentialTimestamp = block.timestamp always results in maximum credential validity for that provider's TTL

transformations: scaledAmount multiplied by scaleFactor via rayMul
data_flows.trust_changes: Market-provided scaleFactor and scaledAmount are trusted for minimum deposit calculation
exposure_risks: If market provides manipulated scaleFactor, minimum deposit check could be bypassed
manipulation_risks: Minimum deposit is checked against normalized amount, which depends on market-supplied scaleFactor

transformations: temporaryReserveRatio = max(2 * relativeDiff, originalReserveRatio) bounded by 10000
sinks: Return values used by market to set state
data_flows.trust_changes: Market contract is trusted as msg.sender. Borrower indirectly controls parameters through market.
validation_points: canExpire requires expiry passed AND rate not decreased
manipulation_risks: Borrower can step-down APR repeatedly: reduce 25%, wait 2 weeks, let expire, reduce another 25% from new baseline, avoiding high temporary reserve ratios

data_flows.source: Lender or anyone calling grantRole / grantRoles
data_flows.trust_changes: Only approved role providers can call. Provider can grant to any account.
manipulation_risks: A provider with type(uint32).max TTL can grant credentials with any timestamp (even 0) since satAdd returns type(uint32).max

core_rules: Only the borrower can deploy markets, manage role providers, block/unblock lenders, and modify market parameters
Lenders must have valid credentials from approved role providers to deposit (when deposit requires access)
Known lenders (who have deposited or received tokens with access) retain withdrawal access even after credential expiry
Fixed term end time can only be decreased, never increased by the borrower
APR reductions trigger temporary increased reserve ratio for 2-week protection period
Credential replacement requires either same provider, unsupported provider, or later expiry
economic_incentives: Borrowers are incentivized to reduce APR to lower interest costs, restrained by temporary reserve ratio increase
Lenders are protected by fixed term restrictions preventing early withdrawal queue
Minimum deposit amounts prevent dust attacks and ensure meaningful participation
Two-week cooling period on APR reductions protects lenders from sudden rate drops
governance_mechanisms: Borrower has unilateral control over role providers, market parameters, and lender blocking
Role providers are external contracts trusted to validate lender credentials
Pull providers can be queried for credential refresh; push providers can only be called by lenders

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should revert with NotHookedMarket since caller is not a registered market
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No isHooked check exists. Function proceeds to _tryValidateAccess, potentially modifying _lenderStatus storage and making external calls to providers via hooksData
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker calls onQueueWithdrawal(victim, ..., maliciousHooksData) to: (1) clear victim's expired credentials from storage, (2) trigger stateful validateCredential calls on providers causing unintended side effects, (3) potentially grief lenders by manipulating their credential state

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Debatable - should there be a minimum remaining term?
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Term is set to 0, immediately allowing all lenders to queue withdrawals since 0 > block.timestamp is false
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Borrower can eliminate fixed term protection at will, potentially front-running lender deposits that expected a minimum term guarantee. Lenders have no on-chain assurance of minimum term beyond trusting the borrower.

edge_cases.scenario: Borrower reduces APR by exactly 25% repeatedly with 2-week waits
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Temporary reserve ratio should protect lenders from drastic rate reductions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each 25% reduction from the NEW baseline avoids elevated reserve ratio since relativeDiff <= 2500 returns originalReserveRatioBips. After expiry, the new lower rate becomes the baseline for the next reduction.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Borrower can reduce APR from 10000 → 7500 → 5625 → 4219 → 3164 over 8 weeks (4 reductions) without ever triggering elevated reserve ratios above original, effectively dropping APR ~68% while maintaining minimum reserves

edge_cases.scenario: Provider added by borrower as non-pull provider, then borrower tries to use it as pull provider in _handleHooksData
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Credential should not be pulled from non-pull provider
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In _handleHooksData with hooksData.length == 20, the check `provider.isPullProvider()` correctly prevents pulling. But if hooksData.length > 20, _tryValidateCredential is called which uses `call` (not staticcall), allowing stateful validation regardless of pull/push status. This is by design but inconsistent.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Non-pull providers can still be invoked via validateCredential path with > 20 bytes of hooksData

edge_cases.scenario: All pull providers removed while lender has canRefresh = true
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _tryValidateAccessInner checks if lastProvider is not null. If provider was removed, _roleProviders[lastProvider] returns 0 (null), so refresh is skipped. _loopTryGetCredential with empty _pullProviders returns false. Credential is unset. Correct behavior.

If tmp.expiry > 0 and annualInterestBips >= 0 (always true for uint16), canExpire might be true if expiry passed. If no tmp exists, the code checks annualInterestBips < originalAnnualInterestBips (0), which is false for uint16 since minimum is 0. No temporary ratio created. Correct.

edge_cases.scenario: HooksConfig.onBorrow assembly uses RepayHook_Base_Size instead of BorrowHook_Base_Size
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses RepayHook_Base_Size (0x0224) which happens to equal BorrowHook_Base_Size (0x0224). No functional impact.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: None currently - cosmetic bug. If constants diverge in future, could cause incorrect calldata sizing.

edge_cases.scenario: Lender with isBlockedFromDeposits=true receives market tokens via transfer on a market where transferRequiresAccess=false
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Blocked lender should not receive tokens
edge_cases.actual_behavior: onTransfer checks isBlockedFromDeposits and reverts if true, even when transferRequiresAccess is false. The block check is applied before the access requirement check. Correct.

edge_cases.scenario: FixedTermLoanHooks._onCreateMarket with hooksData between 32 and 64 bytes
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should either read minimumDeposit or not, clearly
edge_cases.actual_behavior: minimumDeposit is only read if hooksData.length == 64 exactly. For lengths 33-63, only fixedTermEndTime is read. This is correct but could lead to user error if they provide partial data.

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple markets sharing same FixedTermLoanHooks/AccessControlHooks instance with different access requirements
edge_cases.expected_behavior: _lenderStatus is global across all markets, isKnownLenderOnMarket is per-market
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Credential stored in _lenderStatus is shared. A credential granted for market A also works for market B if both use the same hooks. isKnownLenderOnMarket correctly tracks per-market status.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Lender approved for one market automatically has credential for all markets using same hooks instance, even if the borrower intended different access policies per market

assumption_violations: AccessControlHooks.onQueueWithdrawal assumes msg.sender is always a hooked market - no validation performed
fixedTermEndTime validation assumes block.timestamp fits in uint32 (valid until 2106)
Credential system assumes role providers are non-malicious and return meaningful timestamps
_tryGetCredential assembly uses scratch space (0x00-0x3f) which is safe but could conflict with Solidity compiler's free memory usage patterns in edge cases
Temporary reserve ratio mechanism assumes borrower cannot front-run lender withdrawals after reducing APR to 0
game_theory_risks: Borrower can gradually reduce APR by 25% every 2 weeks without elevated reserve ratio penalty, eventually reaching near-zero rates
Borrower can set fixedTermEndTime to 0 to allow immediate withdrawals, potentially after depositing was encouraged at higher rates
Role provider can be malicious and grant/revoke credentials strategically to benefit the borrower
Borrower controls both role providers and blocking, giving full power to gatekeep deposits and restrict specific lenders
integration_risks: External role providers may have bugs or be malicious - all provider calls use try-catch semantics (assembly call returning false on failure)
validateCredential on providers is stateful (CALL not STATICCALL), meaning provider state can change during hooks execution
Market contracts trust hook return values for reserve ratio and interest rate - incorrect hook implementation affects all depositors
If a role provider's isPullProvider() changes behavior after being registered, the hooks contract will use stale pull/push classification
calculation_errors: HooksConfig.onBorrow uses RepayHook_Base_Size (0x0224) instead of BorrowHook_Base_Size (0x0224) - values are equal so no actual error, but a maintenance hazard

token_semantics.notes: Rebasing market tokens where scaleFactor inflates balances. scaledAmount * scaleFactor = normalizedAmount. Used in deposit amount validation via rayMul.

invariants.name: known_lender_immutability
invariants.description: Once set to true, isKnownLenderOnMarket can never be set back to false. Known lenders always retain withdrawal access.

invariants.name: fixed_term_monotonic_decrease
invariants.description: fixedTermEndTime can only be decreased or kept the same, never increased after market creation.

invariants.name: credential_single_owner
invariants.description: Each lender has at most one active credential at a time, from a single provider.

invariants.name: temporary_reserve_original_invariant
invariants.description: Original values are set once when temporary ratio is created and remain unchanged until deleted. New reductions reference these originals, not intermediate values.

invariants.name: pull_provider_index_consistency
invariants.description: pullProviderIndex in _roleProviders must match the actual index in _pullProviders array. Maintained by _removePullProvider swap-and-pop pattern.
